behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR." Slot B: handoff boundary (git-derived branch facts + PR-state provider + staleness evaluator). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, a real bug where commit-age and PR-state wiring are combined incorrectly (e.g., OR vs AND across modules) is missed. Integration-brake check: (a) no explicit real OS/network/subprocess requirement in behavior text; (b) no AI/ML output dependence; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no LLM-stage orchestration. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text does not require "live" or real external infra.
behaviors.test_description: Create an in-process integration test wiring branch-facts module, PR-state adapter (stubbed responses), and staleness evaluator. Use fixture branch states (ahead of main, age over threshold, no PR) and assert stale=true only when all three conditions are satisfied.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning... when the current branch is stale." Slot B: handoff boundary (bootstrap/session hook + staleness result + warning renderer/context formatter). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, a real failure where bootstrap invokes detector but drops branch context in emitted warning is missed. Integration-brake check: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess in text; (b) no AI/ML dependence; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no LLM orchestration. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior asks about emitted warning content, not real process/OS startup semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Run session-start setup in a test harness with injected stale-branch state and verify emitted warning includes actionable fields (branch name, age, ahead/PR context) and only triggers for stale current branch.

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text explicitly scopes to a "local status-classifier function" with deterministic label mapping; failure boundary is one function/object with no required module wiring or I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven unit test for classifier inputs to outputs, including edge values, asserting deterministic mapping to exactly `clean` or `stale-no-pr`.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "against live repo and PR state". Slot B: real-infra boundary (real git/repo state plus real PR source over network and command rendering path). Slot C: if lowered to Integration, mocked repo/PR responses can pass while real command fails due to actual git/HTTP state mismatch or real API field differences. Rejection-gate: rejected Agentic because correctness does not depend on AI/ML model judgment; this is deterministic infra/state evaluation.
behaviors.test_description: Execute `/kaizen-wip` end-to-end in a real test repo with actual branch divergence and real PR state source, then assert rendered output switches between `clean` and `STALE` based on true live conditions.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "open or merged PRs are not flagged... false positives." Slot B: handoff boundary (PR status normalization + stale classifier decision). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, a real bug where merged/open statuses are mis-mapped between modules (e.g., merged treated as no-PR) is missed. Integration-brake check: (a) behavior text does not require live network/API; (b) no AI/ML output dependence; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no LLM orchestration. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior does not require live PR backend, only correct handling of status categories.
behaviors.test_description: Wire PR-status adapter output into staleness classification with fixtures for `open`, `merged`, and `none`; assert only `none` may become `stale-no-pr`, while open/merged remain non-stale.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change". Slot B: real-infra boundary (real git history mutation/subprocess semantics). Slot C: if lowered to Integration, synthetic/mocked commit graphs may miss real git ancestry and rewritten-hash edge cases, causing stale misclassification after rebase/squash. Rejection-gate: rejected Agentic because no AI/ML decision is involved; failure is git state semantics.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repository, create branch commits, perform rebase and squash-merge scenarios that rewrite hashes, then run staleness detection end-to-end and assert branch is not reported as unPRed stale when state is effectively integrated.
